item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic post! This is one my favorite Factory skips although it is a little friendly for that goi. I would have liked to see the flavor list expanded to include a few more Factory appropriate flavors but the creation method for the gum definitely has their flavor. Also the SCP-1200-58 entry reminds me of SCP-2571 which is awesome. I know it more than likely didn't exist when this was originally written but it made me think of it. +1 all the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one, but I also agree with a few other people, it seems more like a wondertainment skip and the metal men thing just confused me. The phone call bit could be better (maybe I'm missing some lore but I didn't understand that, but maybe thats the point.)

However, I really liked the article and the idea for the gum seemed really great.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This seems too… nice to be factory to me. Really seems more wondertainment-like. It's got a sort of playful feel to it.

+1 cause I like it anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first, I didn't quite get how the psychopomps related to this, or 1511 or 1284, but after reading anqxyr's author commentary on his author page, I realize that that was the point. The Factory has its hands in so much that even seemingly unrelated anomalies can succumb to its influence, including the psychopomps. This shows how menacing the Factory really is, and earns my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Remove the death caused by rabbits and this could be a Wondertainment Scip, which is definetely not bad.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Meh… At first I thought this was more or less an extreme version of Bertie Bott's Everyflavor bubblegum. The iron guys are interesting enough to keep me from downvoting - this aspect makes me wonder about a backstory where they were originally a mistake effect and have now taken over the gum Factory in an attempt to make more of themselves. Otherwise the iron guys feel shoehorned in or unconnected, and without them it's just another packet of loosely-related anomalous effects that I would downvote for lack of cohesion.

I did like the last line of the Rubik's flavor, though. Nice body horror in that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm just sad that there's no flavor entitled, "Tastes Like Burning."

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I need like twenty packets of 'tastes like lemons'. Can't get enough of that pucker. Mmmm lemons.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Was Addendum 1200-2 just to add some squick?

Because it worked.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, it absolutely should be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Packages of SCP-1200 periodically appear in various stories across the United States.

Should this be "stores"?

Other than that and some issues with tone that I can't quite put my finger on, this one's really intriguing. Still wrapping my head around the paint-dudes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Taste Like Bubblegum" brand bubblegum. Heh.

item.title: Minor update.
item.content:encoded:
After some consideration, I've decided to make the cross-links here into actual cross-links. So now they are.

They probably aren't in-universe though. At least in my head-canon, the Foundation hasn't figured out the connection there. *shrugs*.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, what color is "dim blue" supposed to be? I usually think of "dim" is referring to lights, not pigments. I'd go with "pale blue" or "dark blue" or something like that, depending on what it is you meant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but the last paragraph of Addendum 1200-2 is confusing as heck to me. It sounds like the paint-men just puke paint-goo onto the corpse until it liquefies and vanishes. How does that result in a new paint-man? Am I missing something there?

EDIT: Sorry, I just realized that they're trying to make paint-gum, not more paint-men. But I still think the paragraph should make it clear that the end result is a stick of gum, or whatever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like gray, only more bleak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What would 'colorless' mean? Transparent like glass?
'cause I don't think they mean 'natural colour', since there's no real way to determine the kinda-gum's natural colour in this case, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't remember the last time I saw a Factory skip. This is some really classic stuff!

the subject stated that he was transported to a green meadow, where he met his younger brother. Notably, the subject does not have any living or deceased siblings.

And this is where it gets amazing. Heartily +1'd!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Morton spotted some nanohives

Not sure if that's a reference…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for making me look up "leporine".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: tastes like upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did the Factory… Did the Factory just burn the Foundation?

Also, I'm just assuming Kurt Cobain had the sole rights to SCP-1200-'91 "Tastes Like Teen Spirit," never released.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I like the idea… It doesn't really mesh with my own view of The Factory. Nonetheless, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do love this, but the wrought iron guys still feel like they don't quite mesh with the gum flavors and such. Still enjoy it regardless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
New skip.

Thanks to Roget, Crayne, and Pig Catapult, for their valuable feedback. Also anyone else whom I forgot. Thanks to Bright for writing 693, which was the inspiration for this.

There are 2 easter-eggs in this article. Not anymore.
